verdict_rationale: Iter-1 resolves OBJ-2 and OBJ-3 cleanly. OBJ-1 is only partially resolved: the negative anchors fail today as designed, but the positive must_contain_all check remains gameable because 4 of 5 positive literals already exist in the schema block at test-writer.md:80-127. Codex independently confirmed Dev could delete the 5 forbidden sentences and add 'root index' as a bare comment, satisfying the AC without performing the Section 7 P1.2 'in lockstep' Procedure/Output/Checklist rewrite. Additionally, 2 NEW spec_text_vs_execution_drift defects surfaced from prose↔JSON divergence: ticket prose for agents/qa.md sub-check mentions only 1 of 2 must_not_contain entries; ticket prose for commands/dev.md sub-check is vague vs the AC JSON's specific must_not_contain on line 632 + must_contain_all 'all three'. Finally, the canonical ticket path docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md has been overwritten AGAIN by the D+H BA cycle during this QA session (file mtime 2026-05-20T06:48Z, my QA session started ~06:43Z) — Cycle 2 ticket prose now lives only in docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515-CYCLE2-round2.md backup.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.details: Three drift defects found, one BLOCKER and two MAJOR. The BLOCKER (AC-C2-02 test-writer.md sub-check) allows Dev to leave the Section 7 P1.2 procedural-drift remediation unimplemented while still passing the AC mechanically. See objections array for prescriptive AC repairs.

objections.title: AC-C2-02 must_contain_all check on agents/test-writer.md passes trivially against CURRENT (untouched) file
objections.evidence: Ran BA's exact check pattern against current untouched test-writer.md: grep for tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json exits 0 (matches at :80, :82, :90); grep for 'kind: index' exits 0 (matches at :119); grep for 'tasks: [' exits 0 (matches at :120). ALL THREE literals are present in the schema block at lines 80-127, which BA itself verified is already Option (b)-correct. The drift BA identified (codex F3: procedural sentences at lines 139/181/183/198/216 still cite ROOT manifest.json as the active store) is NOT enforced by this check.
objections.consequence: Dev could leave test-writer.md procedural sentences completely unmodified and AC-C2-02 would falsely PASS for the test-writer.md sub-check. The binding Section 7 P1.2 remediation Update test-writer.md AND qa.md AND commands/dev.md verification step in lockstep is unenforced for the test-writer.md axis.
objections.proposed_fix: Replace AC-C2-02 test-writer.md must_contain_all literal-grep check with a procedural-text alignment check that is failing TODAY. (a) Add NEGATIVE checks (must_not_contain) for current procedural lines: line 139 wording about Check existing manifest at tests/generated/manifest.json; line 183 about Write tests/generated/manifest.json; line 198 manifest_path: tests/generated/manifest.json in the report template. (b) Add POSITIVE procedural checks: must_contain at least one sentence referring to the root manifest as 'index' (not active store) in the Procedure section (lines 174-186), AND at least one sentence referring to tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json as the active per-task manifest in the same Procedure section. (c) Regenerate ac_uid since the check object changes.
objections.spec_clause: spec-20260518-225715.md Section 7 P1.2 + codex F3 in context.json codex_consult.findings[2]
objections.codex_concurrence: Codex sustained as BLOCKER; independently proposed identical fix (negative checks against old wording + positive checks against new wording).

objections.title: AC-C2-02 qa.md must_contain_all literal tasks[] is brittle -- token presence does not enforce Phase 5 Option (b) consumption
objections.evidence: BA qa.md sub-check requires literals tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json, tasks[], pytest tests/generated/<task_id>/. Running the literal check against current qa.md returns 0 matches (good -- fails today). But the chosen sentinel tasks[] is a bare bracket pair that could be inserted into qa.md prose ANYWHERE WITHOUT actually changing Phase 5 to read root index then find current task_id entry then read that entry manifest_path then verify only the per-task manifest active_tests[]. Current qa.md:992-998 still iterates root active_tests[] directly and runs unscoped pytest tests/generated/ -q. The codex F4 intent (QA scoped to current task_id) and codex F6 intent (schema fields root_index_path + per_task_manifest_path + task_id) are NOT enforced by the brittle literal sentinel.
objections.consequence: Dev could add tasks[] in unrelated prose and leave Phase 5 logic unscoped -- codex F4 regression risk reappears on the FIRST cycle that has test-writer trigger fire.
objections.proposed_fix: Replace the qa.md sub-check with a cohesive multi-element check that enforces the actual codex F4 + F6 intent: (a) must_contain_all should require literals root_index_path AND per_task_manifest_path (binding to F6 schema fields); (b) must_contain literal pytest tests/generated/<task_id>/ OR equivalent scoped form (binding to F4 scoping); (c) must_contain a phrase like current task_id or task_id matches in the Phase 5 section (binding F4 intent); (d) ADD must_not_contain checks for OLD shape: pytest tests/generated/ -q (current line 995, must be removed) and instructions to iterate root manifest active_tests[] directly. Regenerate ac_uid.
objections.spec_clause: spec-20260518-225715.md Section 7 P1.2 + codex F4/F6 in context.json codex_consult.findings[3] and [5]
objections.codex_concurrence: Codex sustained as MAJOR; independently proposed cohesive multi-element check with positive AND negative literals.

objections.title: AC-C2-01 prose-vs-machine drift: 'then' text claims 6 entries plus the four stream stubs and tmp but the JSON contains_all value lists only 5
objections.evidence: AC-C2-01 'then' field reads: allowed_write_path_prefixes contains all six required entries (*/tests/generated/, */docs/dev/test-writer-report-, */.claude/specs/*/cp-state-test-writer.json, */docs/dev/specs/*/cp-state-test-writer.json, */.claude/dev-registry/*/test-writer.json, plus the four stream stubs and tmp). The contains_all value array has only 5 entries -- missing the stream stubs (*/dev/null, */dev/stderr, */dev/stdout) and tmp (/tmp/*, /var/tmp/*) entries that other roles uniformly include. A Dev who reads the JSON check and not the prose could add the 5 listed entries and skip the stream/tmp stubs; AC-C2-01 would pass; test-writer would still fail at runtime for stream redirection scenarios that other roles support.
objections.consequence: Inconsistency between human-readable spec and machine check; Dev could deliver a test-writer role missing operational stubs without AC catching it. BA must decide intent.
objections.proposed_fix: BA chooses one branch and aligns prose with machine: (a) PREFERRED tight scope per Section 7 verbatim -- update 'then' prose to all five required entries and remove plus the four stream stubs and tmp parenthetical; OR (b) full-operability scope -- 'then' prose stays at six-plus-stubs AND contains_all value adds the missing entries (*/dev/null, */dev/stderr, */dev/stdout, /tmp/*, /var/tmp/*). Either branch acceptable; current divergence is not. Also fix the arithmetic in the prose ('all six' but lists more items). Regenerate ac_uid.
objections.spec_clause: spec-20260518-225715.md Section 7 P1.1. Section 7 itself does not enumerate stream/tmp stubs -- BA textual claim went beyond Section 7.
objections.codex_concurrence: Codex sustained as MAJOR; nuance: do not overstate as Section 7 blocker until BA chooses intent. Severity set to major per codex nuance.

observations_only.observation: AC-C2-04 includes .claude/INDEX.md in the grep scope, but that file currently has 0 agent-scores matches. The grep over that file passes trivially today. Not blocking -- Section 7 P2.4 explicitly names .claude/INDEX.md as one of the three indices to regenerate, so AC including it preserves spec-named coverage.

AC-C2-08 grep pattern source.language|source-language uses a regex dot that matches any character. Both source language (space) and source-language (hyphen) and even sourceXlanguage would match. Intended permissiveness -- fine.

observations_only.general_observation: BA overall analysis quality is strong: every evidence claim reproducible, every line number accurate, scope cleanly bounded to Section 7 P1+P2+P3, P4 prohibitions explicit in blast-radius-map. The 3 drift defects in objections are spec_text_vs_execution_drift -- fixable via AC-text-and-recipe edits without changing the cycle prescriptive work. Once OBJ-1, OBJ-2, OBJ-3 are repaired and ac_uids regenerated, the BA deliverable can pass to Dev.
